item.text: I’ll verify the repaired P3F-PET ordering and single-rank execution rule across all three current controls, checking that source production is pre-nominal while endpoint retraining remains post-nominal.

item.aggregated_output:
1	# Publication completion runbook
     2	
     3	**Instructions only; never a run receipt.** Current completion state belongs in
     4	`OPEN_ITEMS.md` and the dimensional STATUS files, verified numbers in
     5	`../VALIDATION_LEDGER.md`, bugs in `../KNOWN_ISSUES.md`, and chronology in the
     6	RUN_LOGs. This runbook defines gates and packet boundaries only.
     7	
     8	No packet below is authorized until PG0 ownership and durability pass: every
     9	input campaign needed by that packet must have a scoped commit, and no result
    10	may be consumed before its summary, ledger entry, RUN_LOG entry, and STATUS
    11	one-liner land in the same commit as its launcher/code. An uncommitted artifact
    12	is provisional even if it is complete on disk.
    13	
    14	## Locked estimator decisions
    15	
    16	These are preconditions, not choices left to an executing worker.
    17	
    18	1. Scalar FPS/N-D production uses explicit `--bkg-mode negweight-refined`.
    19	   Every launcher, manifest, summary, output namespace, and covariance component
    20	   must record that mode. Purity products are matched controls only.
    21	2. Publication PET uses estimator ID `pet-fullevent-fps-v1` and literal aligned
    22	   background-cloud injection at negative POT-scaled `w_bkg`, followed by the
    23	   Stay-Positive refinement. `pet-reduced-fps-cross` and all recoil-only PET
    24	   products are cross-checks, not sources for the publication covariance.
    25	3. PET statistical replicas obey F7: draw coherent Poisson factors over the
    26	   complete data, signal-MC, and background-MC inventories before any training
    27	   subset is selected; apply the background factor before each replica's
    28	   Stay-Positive refinement; reuse every applicable factor exactly at training
    29	   and extraction; persist seeds, factors or replayable factor hashes, and full
    30	   inventory manifests. Nominal refined weights may not be reused by a replica.
    31	4. PET vertical, flux, and detector components use complete joint physical
    32	   variation plus retraining where the nuisance can change the learned map.
    33	   There is no additive `C_syst(frozen map) + C_retrain` construction for the
    34	   same nuisance.
    35	
    36	The locked background decision is indexed in
    37	`../2d-unfolding/HANDOFF_bkg_negweight/bkg_negweight_state.md`; the PET feature
    38	and joint-UQ contract is in
    39	`../nd-unfolding/pet/FULL_EVENT_FEATURE_CONTRACT.md`.
    40	
    41	## Execution preflight for every packet
    42	
    43	Before launch, resume, merge, or adoption:
    44	
    45	1. Read `../KNOWN_ISSUES.md`,
    46	   `../2d-unfolding/2D_OMNIFOLD_REFERENCE.md`, the relevant STATUS and production
    47	   tracker, and `RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md`. PET work also reads
    48	   `../nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md` and the full-event contract.
    49	2. Record source commit, dirty-path ownership, input manifests, estimator and
    50	   background-mode fingerprints, target namespace, and output validation
    51	   command. Never stage another session's changes.
    52	3. Inspect `squeue`, `sacct`, and existing allocation receipts. Decide from live
    53	   evidence whether the work belongs on an existing owner-held interactive node
    54	   or in batch. Use interactive for a single-node task that fits the remaining
    55	   wall; queue dependency-safe arrays, multi-hour, multi-node, GPU, or
    56	   shell-independent work early. Never launch two writers for one namespace.
    57	4. Refuse a nonempty output target unless a content validator proves it is the
    58	   exact completed unit expected by the current manifest. A size-only
    59	   `skip-if-exists` is forbidden. Producers write to a unique temporary path and
    60	   atomically rename only after validation.
    61	5. Use the installed event-loop binary, per-playlist execution, and the canonical
    62	   environment. Do not use a build-tree binary or a combined MEFHC event-loop
    63	   manifest.
    64	6. Keep old, control, and candidate outputs in distinct namespaces. Promotion is
    65	   a validated adoption action, never an overwrite or relabel.
    66	
    67	## Packet PG0 — ownership, provenance, and control freeze
    68	
    69	**Purpose:** make the plan itself durable before dependent production.
    70	
    71	- Attribute every dirty/untracked campaign path to an owner.
    72	- Reconcile completed campaigns one at a time; do not rerun physics to repair a
    73	  missing summary.
    74	- Commit the locked negweight/PET decisions and the canonical OPEN_ITEMS/STATUS
    75	  indexes before a descendant relies on them.
    76	- Independent gate: the publication-plan verifier must return PASS on this
    77	  runbook, the dependency map, and the PET remediation DAG.
    78	
    79	**Output:** scoped control commit(s), with no scientific promotion.
    80	
    81	## Packet P3S — selection-complete standard lateral endpoints
    82	
    83	**Purpose:** replace only the support-limited standard lateral component used by
    84	the scalar 5D chain.
    85	
    86	- Promote each declared kinematic endpoint through selection, IDs,
    87	  backgrounds, native misses, merge, and unfold.
    88	- Require an exact endpoint inventory, asymmetric endpoint identity, universe
    89	  metadata, central/config fingerprint, nonempty-tree checks, and a hardened
    90	  content validator.
    91	- Existing central, vertical, statistical, ML, and corrected joint-throw
    92	  components are read-only inputs after their hashes and summaries validate.
    93	
    94	**Output:** a committed standard lateral-component packet. It does not itself
    95	adopt final 5D or 4D covariance.
    96	
    97	## Packet P3F-scalar — scalar FPS `negweight-refined` production
    98	
    99	**Purpose:** build the scalar FPS central and selection-complete endpoints on the
   100	locked background footing.
   101	
   102	- Existing purity-footed FPS central/component/unfold files are quarantined
   103	  matched controls. Their event-loop inputs may be reused only if a validator
   104	  proves they contain the required signal, data, background, universe,
   105	  phase-space, and provenance information.
   106	- Every unfold command explicitly passes `--bkg-mode negweight-refined`; no
   107	  reliance on a default is allowed.
   108	- Use a separate mode-stamped namespace and atomic output protocol. The manifest
   109	  includes mode, phase-space edges, source ROOT hashes/content fingerprints,
   110	  endpoint identities, estimator seed/config, and expected output set.
   111	- Validate the Stay-Positive telemetry, target normalization, finite weights,
   112	  reported-bin order, completeness, endpoint pairing, and exact inventory.
   113	
   114	**Output:** committed scalar FPS central and component packets on the
   115	`negweight-refined` footing. Purity artifacts remain controls.
   116	
   117	## Packet G2 — full-event binary ownership and FPS-CV regeneration
   118	
   119	**Purpose:** create the publication PET input schema without racing active C++
   120	owners.
   121	
   122	- Wait for all event-loop jobs using the frozen binary to drain and for the C++
   123	  owner to hand off the file. Apply one coordinated branch/interface change and
   124	  one installed-binary rebuild.
   125	- Regenerate per-playlist FPS CV full-event point-cloud ROOTs with the extended
   126	  FPS gate and the background clouds, scalars, keys, `w_bkg`, muon/event
   127	  features, masks/types, and provenance required by
   128	  `pet-fullevent-fps-v1`.
   129	- Prove event-key and row alignment across data, signal, truth, and background;
   130	  validate misses, denominator, reconstructed selection, exact extended edges,
   131	  schema parity, no truth leakage in step 1, and installed-binary identity.
   132	- The present reduced/recoil `xps2` inputs remain scaffolding/cross-checks.
   133	
   134	**Output:** committed full-schema FPS CV roots/inputs and a frozen estimator
   135	fingerprint. Neither PET nominal nor PET laterals may precede this packet.
   136	
   137	## Packet P3F-PET — full-schema shifted PET inputs
   138	
   139	**Purpose:** provide selection-complete detector endpoints for the same
   140	publication PET estimator.
   141	
   142	- Starts only after G2 and a committed P3F-scalar interface inventory.
   143	- Regenerate shifted full-event clouds with the G2 schema; do not attach reduced
   144	  clouds to a full-schema fingerprint.
   145	- Require the same estimator feature schema, preprocessing, extended edges,
   146	  background mode, key ordering, and input fingerprint as the publication CV,
   147	  differing only by the declared endpoint.
   148	- Before any publication nominal launch, require the exact five-band by
   149	  two-endpoint by twelve-playlist inventory, event/cloud/background joins,
   150	  migration census, hashes, schema identity, content validation, and commit
   151	  receipt.
   152	- P3S standard outputs and purity-footed FPS outputs are regression controls
   153	  only.
   154	
   155	**Output:** committed, complete P3F-PET endpoint manifest and validated shifted
   156	inputs.
   157	
   158	## Packet P4 — scalar covariance adoption
   159	
   160	### P4-5D
   161	
   162	Assemble the final scalar 5D covariance from the already committed, validated
   163	non-lateral components plus the P3S lateral replacement. The adoption packet
   164	must contain:
   165	
   166	- common central, reported-bin mask/order, estimator/background fingerprint,
   167	  and component inventory;
   168	- pre/post hashes proving no frozen component changed;
   169	- exact block-sum/reconstruction checks, symmetry, PSD/eigen diagnostics,
   170	  finite diagonal, and mean-shift records;
   171	- tracked product summary plus ledger, RUN_LOG, and STATUS updates in the same
   172	  commit.
   173	
   174	### P4-4D
   175	
   176	Do not rerun the corrected R1 4D throws or non-lateral components. Replace only
   177	the selection-complete lateral component after P3S, then validate and re-adopt
   178	the independent 4D covariance. If publication instead uses the exact 5D
   179	marginal, label the independent 4D result as a cross-check.
   180	
   181	### P4-FPS
   182	
   183	Assemble scalar FPS covariance only from `negweight-refined`, mode-stamped
   184	components produced or explicitly validated under P3F-scalar. No purity central
   185	or purity covariance component may enter the adoption packet.
   186	
   187	**Output:** separate committed adoption packets for each promoted estimator.
   188	
   189	## Packet P5A — publication PET nominal and floor
   190	
   191	**Prerequisites:** PG0, G2, the complete committed P3F-PET source manifest,
   192	frozen `pet-fullevent-fps-v1` contract, and a validated literal-negweight target
   193	builder. P3F-PET source production/validation precedes the nominal; endpoint
   194	retraining and covariance construction follow the nominal.
   195	
   196	Construct the measured target from aligned data events at positive data weight
   197	and aligned background clouds at negative POT-scaled `w_bkg`, then run the
   198	Stay-Positive refinement over the complete nominal target. Persist full
   199	inventories, alignment proof, signed and refined normalization, clipped/floored
   200	telemetry, and background-mode fingerprint.
   201	
   202	Train and extract the full-event nominal; freeze its estimator fingerprint,
   203	central vector, reported-bin mask/order, and extended-FPS edges. Run one matched
   204	GPU-floor repeat before interpreting ensemble spreads. Ordinary closure,
   205	omitted-muon stress closure, finite/full-coverage weights, exact marginals,
   206	normalization, and cap-sensitivity gates must pass.
   207	
   208	**Output:** committed nominal and floor. No reduced, recoil-only, or purity
   209	component transfers.
   210	
   211	## Packet P5B — publication PET uncertainty campaign
   212	
   213	Every component uses the P5A central/mask/order and exact
   214	`pet-fullevent-fps-v1` fingerprint.
   215	
   216	1. **F7 `C_stat`:** generate complete data/signal/background Poisson draws
   217	   before subsetting. Apply background factors before per-replica Stay-Positive;
   218	   use the exact applicable MC/background factors in training and extraction;
   219	   persist replay evidence and reject incomplete inventories. Center on the
   220	   replica mean. Each replica is an independent single-rank job; Horovod and
   221	   distributed rank slicing are prohibited. A pilot may test execution, but
   222	   publication inventory size is predeclared before viewing the covariance.
   223	2. **`C_ML`:** no Poisson variation. Use a predeclared crossed seed design and
   224	   compare with the P5A floor.
   225	3. **Vertical/flux `C_syst`:** for every nuisance that can alter the learned
   226	   mapping, vary physical inputs and retrain jointly, then build covariance from
   227	   those complete joint displacements. A frozen-map-only exception requires an
   228	   explicit proof that the nuisance cannot change the mapping. Never add a
   229	   separate retraining covariance for the same nuisance.
   230	4. **Selection-complete `C_lateral`:** use only P3F-PET shifted full-schema
   231	   inputs; perform the endpoint's physical variation, background refinement,
   232	   retraining, and extraction jointly.
   233	5. **Assembly:** declare nuisance ownership and independence/coupling before
   234	   summation. Require exact component reconstruction, mean shifts, symmetry,
   235	   PSD/eigen diagnostics, finite diagonal, and exact 5D-to-4D marginal checks.
   236	
   237	The ordered targeted-versus-full universe decision is defined in
   238	`../nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md`; it may reduce unnecessary work
   239	but may not weaken the joint-universe contract.
   240	
   241	**Output:** committed PET component packets and final adoption packet. There is
   242	no standalone additive `C_retrain` block.
   243	
   244	## Packet P6 — projections and covariance-dependent analysis
   245	
   246	Starts only from committed P4/P5B adoption summaries.
   247	
   248	- Rebuild exact 2D, 3D, 4D, `(Eavail,W)`, and declared FPS/PET marginals using
   249	  explicit projection matrices and bin-volume conventions.
   250	- Validate `M C M^T` against direct block sums, order/mask metadata, symmetry,
   251	  PSD, and normalization.
   252	- Recompute generator comparisons and significances only from the governing
   253	  adopted covariance. Preserve old values as provenance, not live claims.
   254	- PET/scalar comparisons require the same extended FPS measurement domain and
   255	  separate acceptance-supported versus prior-dominated reporting tiers.
   256	
   257	## Packet P7 — publication documents and release freeze
   258	
   259	- Update note, primer, paper, figures, tables, and release manifests only from
   260	  committed summaries and the validation ledger.
   261	- Build all three analysis-note targets and run link/reference/provenance checks.
   262	- Complete blinded/full-author review, collaboration approvals, and external
   263	  questions tracked in `OPEN_ITEMS.md`.
   264	- Tag the immutable publication-results tree only after every cited artifact is
   265	  reachable from a commit and release manifest.
   266	
   267	Post-publication reorganization is outside this runbook and starts only after
   268	that tag.
   269	
   270	## Legacy recoil-PET boundary
   271	
   272	Existing recoil-only nominal, floor, replicas, and covariance products remain a
   273	labeled representation cross-check. Optional completion of its replica
   274	inventory must use a legacy-only namespace and cannot satisfy, feed, or delay
   275	P5A/P5B. Do not rerun completed legacy products merely to repair documentation.
   276	
   277	## Independent final audit
   278	
   279	Before publication synthesis, an independent verifier must confirm:
   280	
   281	- all scalar FPS promoted inputs/components are `negweight-refined` and purity
   282	  is control-only;
   283	- PET uses literal background clouds, Stay-Positive, F7, full-schema G2/P3F,
   284	  and one estimator fingerprint;
   285	- joint PET nuisances contain retraining with no additive double count;
   286	- corrected 4D non-lateral components were not rerun;
   287	- explicit final 5D, 4D, FPS, and PET adoption packets exist;
   288	- every skip was content-validated or atomic; and
   289	- every scientific claim is committed with its canonical ledger/RUN_LOG/STATUS
   290	  evidence.

1	# Result dependency and rerun map
     2	
     3	**Routing instructions only.** This file defines dependency and invalidation
     4	rules; it does not record current completion. Live work belongs in
     5	`OPEN_ITEMS.md` and dimensional STATUS files, verified numbers in
     6	`../VALIDATION_LEDGER.md`, bugs in `../KNOWN_ISSUES.md`, and chronology in the
     7	RUN_LOGs.
     8	
     9	## Node vocabulary
    10	
    11	| Mark | Meaning |
    12	|---|---|
    13	| `REUSE` | Consume only after its commit, summary, content fingerprint, and estimator contract validate. |
    14	| `BUILD` | A new node is required in a distinct namespace. |
    15	| `REPLACE` | Swap only the named component; prove all other component hashes are unchanged. |
    16	| `QUARANTINE` | Preserve as a labeled control/cross-check; never feed a publication descendant. |
    17	| `GATED` | No launch until all named upstream commits and validators pass. |
    18	
    19	No filesystem artifact is implicitly `REUSE`. Size-only existence checks never
    20	establish completion.
    21	
    22	## Dependency graph
    23	
    24	```mermaid
    25	flowchart TD
    26	  PG0[PG0 committed controls and ownership] --> P3S[P3S standard active endpoints]
    27	  PG0 --> P3FS[P3F-scalar negweight-refined FPS]
    28	
    29	  S5[REUSE committed scalar 5D non-lateral components] --> A5[P4-5D adoption]
    30	  P3S --> L5[standard lateral replacement]
    31	  L5 --> A5
    32	
    33	  R4[REUSE corrected R1 4D non-lateral components] --> A4[P4-4D adoption]
    34	  L5 --> A4
    35	
    36	  P3FS --> AFPS[P4-FPS adoption]
    37	
    38	  P3S --> DRAIN[binary ownership and drain gate]
    39	  P3FS --> DRAIN
    40	  DRAIN --> G2[G2 full-schema FPS CV regeneration]
    41	  G2 --> P3FP[P3F-PET full-schema shifted inputs]
    42	  P3FP --> NPET[P5A full-event PET nominal and floor]
    43	  NPET --> F7[F7 coherent stat]
    44	  NPET --> ML[PET ML ensemble]
    45	  NPET --> VS[joint vertical and flux retraining]
    46	  P3FP --> LAT[joint selection-complete lateral retraining]
    47	  F7 --> APET[P5B PET adoption]
    48	  ML --> APET
    49	  VS --> APET
    50	  LAT --> APET
    51	
    52	  A5 --> PROJ[P6 projections and significances]
    53	  A4 --> PROJ
    54	  AFPS --> PROJ
    55	  APET --> PROJ
    56	  PROJ --> DOCS[P7 documents and release freeze]
    57	
    58	  PUR[Purity FPS products] -. QUARANTINE .-> CTRL[matched controls]
    59	  RED[Reduced/recoil PET products] -. QUARANTINE .-> CTRL
    60	```
    61	
    62	## Estimator identities and allowed parents
    63	
    64	| Publication node | Required identity | Allowed parents | Forbidden parents |
    65	|---|---|---|---|
    66	| Scalar 5D covariance | Standard scalar central/mask/order and validated component contract | Committed corrected non-lateral components plus P3S lateral replacement | Purity-footed FPS, support-limited old lateral, mismatched masks/seeds |
    67	| Independent 4D covariance | Corrected R1 4D central/mask/order | Reuse R1 non-lateral components; replace only lateral from P3S | Rerun corrected throws without a new invalidation; silent 5D relabel |
    68	| Scalar FPS covariance | Explicit `negweight-refined`, extended FPS edges, mode-stamped manifest | P3F-scalar central/components only | Purity central/components; standard-phase-space endpoints |
    69	| Publication PET | `pet-fullevent-fps-v1`, literal negative background clouds plus Stay-Positive | G2 CV, P5A, F7, joint systematics, P3F-PET | `pet-reduced-fps-cross`, recoil-only products, purity target, P3S endpoints |
    70	| Legacy recoil PET | Legacy recoil fingerprint | Its own completed or optional legacy-only replicas | Any publication full-event descendant |
    71	
    72	## Scalar 5D and corrected 4D routes
    73	
    74	```text
    75	REUSE validated scalar 5D central + corrected non-lateral components
    76	  + BUILD P3S selection-complete standard endpoints
    77	  -> REPLACE standard lateral only
    78	  -> ADOPT final scalar 5D covariance
    79	  -> PROJECT lower-dimensional covariances and significances
    80	
    81	REUSE corrected R1 independent-4D central + non-lateral components
    82	  + REUSE the validated P3S-derived lateral replacement
    83	  -> REPLACE lateral only
    84	  -> ADOPT independent 4D covariance
    85	```
    86	
    87	The R1 4D throws and other corrected non-lateral components remain reusable
    88	unless their own code/config/input contract changes. A selection-complete
    89	lateral change does not authorize rerunning them. Publication may instead use
    90	the exact 5D-to-4D marginal, but it must label the independent 4D estimator as a
    91	cross-check in that case.
    92	
    93	## Scalar FPS route
    94	
    95	```text
    96	VALIDATE reusable FPS event-loop/universe inputs
    97	  -> BUILD explicit negweight-refined central and endpoint unfolds
    98	     in a mode-stamped atomic namespace
    99	  -> BUILD/VALIDATE compatible statistical, ML, vertical, joint-throw,
   100	     and selection-complete lateral components
   101	  -> ADOPT scalar FPS covariance
   102	```
   103	
   104	All existing purity-footed unfolds, central values, and covariances are
   105	`QUARANTINE` matched controls. Reusing an underlying ROOT input requires proof
   106	that it contains the background and universe information needed to construct
   107	the `negweight-refined` target; it never permits reuse of the purity unfold.
   108	
   109	## Publication PET route
   110	
   111	```text
   112	PG0 controls committed
   113	  -> binary-owner drain gate
   114	  -> G2 full-schema FPS CV ROOT/NPZ with data, signal, truth, and background
   115	     clouds/scalars/keys/weights
   116	  -> freeze pet-fullevent-fps-v1 fingerprint
   117	  -> BUILD literal signed data+background target and Stay-Positive refinement
   118	  -> BUILD nominal + GPU floor
   119	  -> BUILD F7 coherent C_stat over full data/signal/background inventories
   120	  + BUILD PET C_ML with no Poisson variation
   121	  + BUILD joint physical-variation-plus-retraining vertical/flux components
   122	  + BUILD P3F-PET full-schema shifted inputs and joint lateral retraining
   123	  -> ADOPT one PET covariance on one central/mask/order/fingerprint
   124	  -> PROJECT/report acceptance-supported and prior-dominated tiers
   125	```
   126	
   127	G2 precedes both the nominal and fresh full-schema P3F-PET. Reduced P3F or
   128	recoil-only `xps2` artifacts cannot be promoted by changing a label. The exact
   129	five-band by two-endpoint by twelve-playlist P3F-PET source inventory, joins,
   130	migration census, hashes, schema proof, and commit receipt must pass before the
   131	publication nominal starts; only endpoint retraining/extraction follows it.
   132	
   133	For every PET nuisance that can change the learned mapping,
   134	
   135	```text
   136	delta_u = x_u(varied physical input + retrained estimator) - x_CV
   137	```
   138	
   139	and the component is built directly from the declared set of joint shifts. Do
   140	not form a separate additive frozen-map covariance plus retraining covariance
   141	for the same nuisance. Per-universe background target construction repeats the
   142	literal negative injection and Stay-Positive refinement on that universe.
   143	
   144	## F7 coherent statistical route
   145	
   146	For each replica `r`:
   147	
   148	1. Enumerate the full ordered data, signal-MC, and background-MC inventories.
   149	2. Draw and persist coherent Poisson factors before any training subset. Each
   150	   replica executes as an independent single-rank job; Horovod/distributed rank
   151	   slicing is prohibited.
   152	3. Apply data factors to data events, signal factors wherever signal MC enters,
   153	   and background factors to the negative background injection.
   154	4. Run the replica-specific Stay-Positive refinement after those factors.
   155	5. Select the training subset without redrawing or reindexing the global
   156	   factors.
   157	6. Reuse the exact applicable MC/background factors at extraction and prove
   158	   row/key identity.
   159	
   160	The nominal refined target is not a replica input. Missing inventory members,
   161	duplicate keys, seed/factor mismatch, or incomplete extraction fail the whole
   162	replica manifest.
   163	
   164	## Trigger-to-invalidation rules
   165	
   166	| Trigger | Earliest restart | Invalidated descendants | Explicitly unaffected |
   167	|---|---|---|---|
   168	| P3S standard endpoints change | Standard lateral merge | P4-5D/P4-4D lateral and their adoptions/projections | Frozen central and validated non-lateral components |
   169	| Scalar FPS background mode or target changes | P3F-scalar unfold | Scalar FPS central, compatible UQ, adoption, comparisons | Standard scalar chain; legacy recoil PET |
   170	| G2 feature/schema/input changes | G2 CV/interface validation | P5A, P3F-PET, all P5B components and projections | Scalar results; legacy recoil PET |
   171	| PET estimator fingerprint changes | P5A nominal | Floor and all PET UQ/descendants | Scalar results and quarantined cross-checks |
   172	| F7 inventory/draw contract changes | First affected replica | `C_stat`, PET total, PET projections/comparisons | P5A nominal/floor and non-stat components |
   173	| PET nuisance implementation changes | First affected joint universe | Its component, PET total, dependent projections | Other independently owned components after hash proof |
   174	| Projection/bin-volume mapping changes | Projection validator | Projected covariance and derived significances | Upstream adopted covariance |
   175	| Summary/ledger/RUN_LOG/STATUS missing | Provenance commit | Quote/citation status only | Heavy artifact; do not rerun physics |
   176	
   177	## Reuse and atomic-skip proof
   178	
   179	Every `REUSE` or skip record contains:
   180	
   181	- source commit and launcher/config identity;
   182	- exact expected unit inventory and content-level validator result;
   183	- input/output hashes or stable ROOT-content fingerprints;
   184	- estimator, background mode, phase-space edges, mask/order, and seed policy;
   185	- scheduler/job receipt where applicable; and
   186	- proof the artifact is not a partial temporary file.
   187	
   188	Every producer writes to a unique temporary path, validates it, then atomically
   189	renames. An interrupted partial is quarantined and regenerated; a nonzero file
   190	size is never a completion test.
   191	
   192	## Adoption proof
   193	
   194	Final 5D, 4D, scalar FPS, and PET are separate adoption packets. Each packet
   195	must prove common central/mask/order/fingerprint, exact component inventory and
   196	block reconstruction, non-mutation of reused components, symmetry, PSD/eigen
   197	diagnostics, finite diagonal, projection consistency, and canonical commit
   198	evidence. P6 and publication documents depend on these adoption commits, not on
   199	loose component files.
   200	
   201	The executable packet contract is in
   202	[the publication completion runbook](PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md).

1	# Publication PET UQ remediation status and ordered DAG
     2	
     3	**Scope:** the publication full-event PET estimator
     4	`pet-fullevent-fps-v1`. Current completion remains indexed in
     5	`../docs/OPEN_ITEMS.md` and `ND_OMNIFOLD_STATUS.md`; verified numbers belong
     6	only in `../VALIDATION_LEDGER.md`; chronology belongs in
     7	`ND_OMNIFOLD_RUN_LOG.md`.
     8	
     9	This file is an execution gate, not proof that a node passed. The exact feature
    10	schema and joint-UQ definition are in
    11	`pet/FULL_EVENT_FEATURE_CONTRACT.md`; the packet/commit contract is in
    12	`../docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md`.
    13	
    14	## Legacy boundary
    15	
    16	The completed recoil-only PET nominal, floor, ensembles, and covariance are a
    17	non-publication representation cross-check. Existing recoil inputs and scalar
    18	purity-weight targets are not inputs to this DAG. Optional legacy replica work
    19	uses its own namespace and cannot satisfy, feed, or delay any gate below. Do not
    20	rerun completed legacy products to repair documentation.
    21	
    22	The reduced full-event interface estimator `pet-reduced-fps-cross` is likewise
    23	an interface/stress-closure cross-check. It cannot provide a central value,
    24	lateral endpoint, or covariance component for `pet-fullevent-fps-v1`.
    25	
    26	## Gate 0 — durable controls and ownership
    27	
    28	Before any publication PET compute:
    29	
    30	1. The locked background decision, full-event contract, dependency map, and
    31	   runbook are committed and independently verified.
    32	2. G2 has a named C++ owner. Existing event-loop jobs using the current binary
    33	   have drained, the owner has handed off the source, and only one coordinated
    34	   installed-binary rebuild is planned.
    35	3. Output namespaces distinguish reduced/recoil controls, purity controls,
    36	   full-schema candidates, and adopted products.
    37	4. Every launch has an interactive-versus-batch decision based on live queue,
    38	   resource, dependency, remaining-wall, and output-ownership evidence. No two
    39	   jobs may write one namespace.
    40	
    41	**Gate:** scoped control commit and independent publication-plan PASS.
    42	
    43	## Gate 1 — G2 full-schema FPS CV input
    44	
    45	Regenerate the FPS CV point-cloud event loops and derived inputs with the exact
    46	`pet-fullevent-fps-v1` schema. The source must include aligned data, signal MC,
    47	truth MC, and background MC clouds plus event scalars/features, event keys,
    48	masks/types, POT/weights, native misses, extended-FPS edges, and schema/binary
    49	provenance.
    50	
    51	Required proofs:
    52	
    53	- `MNV101_FULL_PHASE_SPACE=1` and the declared full-event dump are embedded and
    54	  verified;
    55	- data/reco observable schema parity and distinct truth schema;
    56	- event-key and row alignment, uniqueness, denominator/miss consistency, and
    57	  reconstructed-selection identity;
    58	- exact extended-FPS edges and reported-bin order;
    59	- no truth-only feature enters step 1;
    60	- background clouds carry the same reco schema needed for data-side injection,
    61	  along with aligned `w_bkg`; and
    62	- full-schema fingerprint differs from the reduced/recoil fingerprints.
    63	
    64	**Gate:** committed G2 roots/inputs, content summary, interface tests, ledger,
    65	RUN_LOG, and STATUS evidence. Present `xps2` recoil tensors remain scaffolding.
    66	
    67	## Gate 2 — literal `negweight-refined` target
    68	
    69	Build the measured-side training inventory from:
    70	
    71	- aligned data events with their positive data weights; and
    72	- aligned background clouds with negative POT-scaled `w_bkg`.
    73	
    74	Apply the Stay-Positive refinement to that complete signed inventory. Do not
    75	copy scalar purity weights, silently downweight only the data rows, or reuse a
    76	nominal refined target in a bootstrap replica.
    77	
    78	The target summary records:
    79	
    80	- ordered data and background manifests and event-key alignment;
    81	- POT scale and raw positive/negative sums;
    82	- refinement configuration/fingerprint;
    83	- refined normalization and clipping/floor telemetry;
    84	- finite/non-negative post-refinement weights; and
    85	- estimator ID, feature schema, extended edges, input hashes, and target mode
    86	  `negweight-refined`.
    87	
    88	**Gate:** an end-to-end fixture reaches training and extraction; signed and
    89	refined targets reproduce their independently constructed binned checks; all
    90	invalid/missing/misaligned background inputs fail closed.
    91	
    92	## Gate 3 — committed full-schema P3F-PET source inventory
    93	
    94	Before the publication nominal, regenerate and validate the complete
    95	selection-shifted source inventory under the G2 full schema: five declared
    96	kinematic bands by two endpoints by twelve playlists. Require exact event,
    97	cloud, and background joins; selection/migration census; native-miss and
    98	denominator evidence; source/input hashes; schema and estimator identity;
    99	endpoint/playlist completeness; atomic content validation; and the scoped
   100	commit receipt.
   101	
   102	P3S standard endpoints, scalar-FPS purity unfolds, reduced-schema P3F, and
   103	uncommitted full-schema files are controls only. This gate produces validated
   104	shifted inputs, not endpoint-trained covariance. The latter follows the nominal
   105	at Gate 8.
   106	
   107	## Gate 4 — nominal and GPU floor
   108	
   109	Train and extract one unbootstrapped publication nominal from the Gate-2 target
   110	using the frozen estimator fingerprint. Freeze its central vector, reported-bin
   111	mask/order, phase-space edges, seed/config policy, and extraction normalization.
   112	
   113	Run one matched repeat to bound GPU nondeterminism before interpreting either
   114	statistical or ML ensembles.
   115	
   116	Required validation includes ordinary closure, the omitted-muon stress closure,
   117	finite full-coverage weights, strict MC index/order, cap-sensitivity telemetry,
   118	normalization, exact lower-dimensional marginals, and the acceptance-supported
   119	versus prior-dominated reporting split.
   120	
   121	**Gate:** committed nominal and floor packets. No UQ component may use a
   122	different central/mask/order/fingerprint.
   123	
   124	## Gate 5 — F7 coherent statistical replicas
   125	
   126	For every replica, in this exact order:
   127	
   128	1. Enumerate complete, ordered data, signal-MC, and background-MC inventories
   129	   before any training subset. Each replica is an independent single-rank job;
   130	   Horovod and distributed rank slicing are prohibited.
   131	2. Draw one coherent Poisson factor per inventory member from a persisted,
   132	   replayable replica seed policy.
   133	3. Apply data factors to data weights, signal factors everywhere signal MC is
   134	   used, and background factors to the negative background injection.
   135	4. Run a fresh Stay-Positive refinement for that replica after applying the
   136	   background factors.
   137	5. Select the training subset without redrawing, shortening, or reindexing the
   138	   full factors.
   139	6. Reuse the exact applicable signal/background factors during full extraction
   140	   and completeness/count construction.
   141	
   142	Persist full inventory hashes, seeds, factor hashes or replayable factors,
   143	subset indices, train/extract identity checks, target/refinement telemetry, and
   144	completion status. A missing unit invalidates the replica; a missing replica
   145	invalidates the declared ensemble manifest. Center `C_stat` on the accepted
   146	replica mean.
   147	
   148	Run a small pilot only to validate the machinery. Declare the publication
   149	replica inventory before viewing its covariance and compare its spread with the
   150	Gate-4 floor.
   151	
   152	## Gate 6 — PET-specific ML ensemble
   153	
   154	Use the nominal target with no Poisson fluctuation. Vary only the predeclared
   155	crossed training/subsample/split and estimator seeds. Persist all seeds and
   156	completed extractions; center on the ensemble mean and compare with the GPU
   157	floor. Scalar GBDT ML ensembles do not substitute for this component.
   158	
   159	## Gate 7 — targeted joint systematic retraining decision
   160	
   161	Publication vertical/flux systematics are joint end-to-end variations. For a
   162	nuisance `u` that can change the learned mapping:
   163	
   164	```text
   165	delta_u = x_u(varied physical input + universe-specific background refinement
   166	              + retrained estimator) - x_CV
   167	```
   168	
   169	The component is constructed directly from the declared joint shifts. Do not
   170	build or add separate frozen-map and retraining covariances for the same
   171	nuisance; they share the nuisance and would omit/corrupt their cross term.
   172	
   173	First run a predeclared targeted set spanning dominant model, flux, and
   174	kinematic behaviors. Compare the joint retrained displacement with the matched
   175	frozen-map diagnostic only to decide expansion. Declare trace and tail
   176	materiality thresholds before looking at results and bound the GPU floor.
   177	
   178	- If the learned-map response is immaterial for a documented nuisance, a
   179	  frozen-map-only treatment is allowed only with the proof and bound in its
   180	  component summary.
   181	- If material for selected bands, expand those complete bands/endpoints.
   182	- If broadly material, expand to the full applicable universe inventory.
   183	
   184	This gate controls cost, not estimator definition: every adopted endpoint is
   185	still a complete joint variation, and every universe repeats the literal
   186	background injection plus Stay-Positive construction.
   187	
   188	## Gate 8 — full-schema selection-complete laterals
   189	
   190	Consumes only the committed Gate-3 P3F-PET source manifest. For every declared
   191	lateral endpoint, carry its shifted selection, event membership, background
   192	target, native misses, and full-event input into joint background refinement,
   193	retraining, and extraction.
   194	
   195	P3S standard endpoints, scalar-FPS purity unfolds, and reduced-schema P3F
   196	artifacts are controls only. Every accepted endpoint must share the nominal
   197	estimator fingerprint and differ only by the declared physical variation.
   198	Build the lateral component from the complete asymmetric endpoint inventory
   199	under the experiment's declared centering convention, with a separate mean
   200	shift record.
   201	
   202	## Gate 9 — adoption and exact projections
   203	
   204	Declare nuisance ownership and independence/coupling before covariance
   205	assembly. On one matched central/mask/order/fingerprint, assemble only:
   206	
   207	```text
   208	C_total = C_stat + C_ML + C_syst_joint + C_lateral_joint
   209	```
   210	
   211	There is no separate additive `C_retrain`. Require exact component/block
   212	reconstruction, common central and ordering, symmetry, PSD/eigen diagnostics,
   213	finite diagonal, mean-shift diagnostics, extended-edge validation, and exact
   214	5D-to-4D covariance projection.
   215	
   216	Adoption also requires acceptance-supported/prior-dominated tier outputs,
   217	coverage and prior-envelope controls, scalar/PET comparison only on a common
   218	measurement domain, tracked product summary, and ledger/RUN_LOG/STATUS entries
   219	in the same commit.
   220	
   221	Until Gate 9 lands, label outputs by component and candidate fingerprint; do
   222	not call them the final PET budget.
   223	
   224	## Reuse/rerun matrix
   225	
   226	| Item | Disposition |
   227	|---|---|
   228	| Recoil-only PET products and copied scalar purity targets | `QUARANTINE` cross-check; no transfer |
   229	| Reduced full-event interface/stress products | `QUARANTINE` interface cross-check; no transfer |
   230	| Extended-edge and reporting conventions | `REUSE` after exact contract/hash validation |
   231	| Raw source AnaTuple inputs and per-playlist manifests | `REUSE` after provenance/availability checks |
   232	| Existing FPS event-loop roots lacking full schema/background clouds | Insufficient for G2; do not relabel |
   233	| G2 full-schema FPS CV roots/NPZ | `BUILD` once after binary drain/ownership gate |
   234	| Literal signed/refined nominal target | `BUILD` from G2 inventories |
   235	| Publication nominal and GPU floor | `BUILD` under one fingerprint |
   236	| `C_stat` | `BUILD` with F7 full data/signal/background draws |
   237	| PET `C_ML` | `BUILD` independently with no Poisson |
   238	| Vertical/flux systematic component | `BUILD` from adopted joint end-to-end universes |
   239	| P3F-PET source inventory | `BUILD` and commit after G2, before nominal |
   240	| P3F-PET endpoint retraining/laterals | `BUILD` after nominal from Gate-3 inputs |
   241	| Full per-universe retraining | `GATED` by targeted materiality, never assumed unnecessary |
   242	
   243	## Atomicity and scheduler gate
   244	
   245	Every unit writes a unique temporary artifact, runs its content validator, and
   246	atomically renames only on success. Resume logic checks manifest completeness,
   247	schema/fingerprint, and content—not file size. Partial artifacts are
   248	quarantined.
   249	
   250	Before each launch, inspect existing allocations and queues. Use an owner-held
   251	interactive node for single-node work that fits its remaining wall; queue
   252	dependency-safe arrays, long/GPU jobs, or work that must outlive the shell early.
   253	Never duplicate a queued/running writer, and never cancel another worker's job
   254	or allocation.

60	```
    61	
    62	## Estimator identities and allowed parents
    63	
    64	| Publication node | Required identity | Allowed parents | Forbidden parents |
    65	|---|---|---|---|
    66	| Scalar 5D covariance | Standard scalar central/mask/order and validated component contract | Committed corrected non-lateral components plus P3S lateral replacement | Purity-footed FPS, support-limited old lateral, mismatched masks/seeds |
    67	| Independent 4D covariance | Corrected R1 4D central/mask/order | Reuse R1 non-lateral components; replace only lateral from P3S | Rerun corrected throws without a new invalidation; silent 5D relabel |
    68	| Scalar FPS covariance | Explicit `negweight-refined`, extended FPS edges, mode-stamped manifest | P3F-scalar central/components only | Purity central/components; standard-phase-space endpoints |
    69	| Publication PET | `pet-fullevent-fps-v1`, literal negative background clouds plus Stay-Positive | G2 CV, P5A, F7, joint systematics, P3F-PET | `pet-reduced-fps-cross`, recoil-only products, purity target, P3S endpoints |
    70	| Legacy recoil PET | Legacy recoil fingerprint | Its own completed or optional legacy-only replicas | Any publication full-event descendant |
    71	
    72	## Scalar 5D and corrected 4D routes
    73	
    74	```text
    75	REUSE validated scalar 5D central + corrected non-lateral components
    76	  + BUILD P3S selection-complete standard endpoints
    77	  -> REPLACE standard lateral only
    78	  -> ADOPT final scalar 5D covariance
    79	  -> PROJECT lower-dimensional covariances and significances
    80	
    81	REUSE corrected R1 independent-4D central + non-lateral components
    82	  + REUSE the validated P3S-derived lateral replacement
    83	  -> REPLACE lateral only
    84	  -> ADOPT independent 4D covariance
    85	```
    86	
    87	The R1 4D throws and other corrected non-lateral components remain reusable
    88	unless their own code/config/input contract changes. A selection-complete
    89	lateral change does not authorize rerunning them. Publication may instead use
    90	the exact 5D-to-4D marginal, but it must label the independent 4D estimator as a
    91	cross-check in that case.
    92	
    93	## Scalar FPS route
    94	
    95	```text
    96	VALIDATE reusable FPS event-loop/universe inputs
    97	  -> BUILD explicit negweight-refined central and endpoint unfolds
    98	     in a mode-stamped atomic namespace
    99	  -> BUILD/VALIDATE compatible statistical, ML, vertical, joint-throw,
   100	     and selection-complete lateral components
   101	  -> ADOPT scalar FPS covariance
   102	```
   103	
   104	All existing purity-footed unfolds, central values, and covariances are
   105	`QUARANTINE` matched controls. Reusing an underlying ROOT input requires proof
   106	that it contains the background and universe information needed to construct
   107	the `negweight-refined` target; it never permits reuse of the purity unfold.
   108	
   109	## Publication PET route
   110	
   111	```text
   112	PG0 controls committed
   113	  -> binary-owner drain gate
   114	  -> G2 full-schema FPS CV ROOT/NPZ with data, signal, truth, and background
   115	     clouds/scalars/keys/weights
   116	  -> freeze pet-fullevent-fps-v1 fingerprint
   117	  -> BUILD literal signed data+background target and Stay-Positive refinement
   118	  -> BUILD nominal + GPU floor
   119	  -> BUILD F7 coherent C_stat over full data/signal/background inventories
   120	  + BUILD PET C_ML with no Poisson variation
   121	  + BUILD joint physical-variation-plus-retraining vertical/flux components
   122	  + BUILD P3F-PET full-schema shifted inputs and joint lateral retraining
   123	  -> ADOPT one PET covariance on one central/mask/order/fingerprint
   124	  -> PROJECT/report acceptance-supported and prior-dominated tiers
   125	```
   126	
   127	G2 precedes both the nominal and fresh full-schema P3F-PET. Reduced P3F or
   128	recoil-only `xps2` artifacts cannot be promoted by changing a label. The exact
   129	five-band by two-endpoint by twelve-playlist P3F-PET source inventory, joins,
   130	migration census, hashes, schema proof, and commit receipt must pass before the
   131	publication nominal starts; only endpoint retraining/extraction follows it.
   132	
   133	For every PET nuisance that can change the learned mapping,
   134	
   135	```text
   136	delta_u = x_u(varied physical input + retrained estimator) - x_CV
   137	```
   138	
   139	and the component is built directly from the declared set of joint shifts. Do
   140	not form a separate additive frozen-map covariance plus retraining covariance
   141	for the same nuisance. Per-universe background target construction repeats the
   142	literal negative injection and Stay-Positive refinement on that universe.
   143	
   144	## F7 coherent statistical route
   145

The dependency graph, runbook, PET DAG, and single-rank rule are repaired, but the map’s textual PET route still places “BUILD P3F-PET full-schema shifted inputs” after the nominal ([line 111](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md:111)), contradicting its corrected graph and explicit pre-nominal rule at [line 127](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md:127).

Smallest correction: move `BUILD/COMMIT P3F-PET full-schema source inventory` before the nominal in that text route; leave only joint endpoint refinement/retraining/extraction after the nominal.
